variants.subject: python
variants.title: Python
variants.tagline: The essential 20% that does 80% of the work

content.overview: A rapid, high-signal session covering the Python concepts that actually matter in practice. No fluff, no padding. By the end you'll be writing real scripts and know exactly where to go next.

sessions.title: The Python Mental Model
topics: The REPL and running your first script

content.prerequisites: None — just a laptop and curiosity
outcomes: Understand how data flows through a program
Know exactly what to learn next

variants.subject: ai
variants.title: AI Demystified
variants.tagline: What AI actually is — and how it actually works

content.overview: Strip away the hype. Understand how machine learning, neural networks, and LLMs actually work — without needing a PhD. Built around visual intuition and live demos throughout.

sessions.title: What Machines Actually Learn
topics: What ML really is (vs. traditional programming)

sessions.title: Neural Networks & LLMs
topics: How a neural network learns — forward & backward pass
How GPT-4 and Claude work under the hood

sessions.title: AI in Practice
topics: Live: building with AI APIs (OpenAI / Anthropic)
Prompt engineering principles that actually work
Where AI fails — hallucinations, bias, limits
Roadmap: how to go deeper into AI

content.prerequisites: Curiosity only. No maths or coding required.
outcomes: Explain how LLMs work to anyone
Understand the real limits of current AI
Use AI APIs to build something real
Know the right learning path to go deeper

variants.subject: maths
variants.title: Calculus
variants.tagline: Derivatives and integrals — actually understood

content.overview: A focused deep-dive into the two big ideas in calculus — derivatives and integrals — built around geometric intuition first and formulas second. You'll leave actually understanding why the rules work.

sessions.title: Derivatives
topics: What a derivative really means — the slope idea
Chain rule, product rule — explained visually

sessions.title: Applications & ML Connections
topics: Where calculus appears in ML (gradient descent)

content.prerequisites: Basic algebra and familiarity with functions (y = f(x))
outcomes: Apply calculus to real optimisation problems

variants.subject: maths
variants.title: Linear Algebra for ML
variants.tagline: The maths behind neural networks — made visual

content.overview: Every AI model is built on linear algebra. This session builds a visual, geometric intuition for vectors, matrices, and transformations — exactly the parts that show up in ML.

sessions.title: Vectors & Spaces
topics: Vector operations — addition, scaling, dot product

sessions.title: Matrices as Transformations
topics: Matrix multiplication as composition of transformations

sessions.title: Eigenvalues, PCA & Neural Nets
topics: How weights in neural networks are matrices

content.prerequisites: Basic algebra. Calculus helpful but not required.
outcomes: Read ML papers without freezing at the maths
Use NumPy confidently for linear algebra

variants.subject: python
variants.title: Python Weekend Intensive
variants.tagline: Go from zero to building projects in a single weekend

content.overview: An immersive two-day cohort that takes you from absolute basics to building three real Python projects. Small group, live instruction, immediate feedback after every block.
content.prerequisites: No programming experience needed
outcomes: Build and ship 3 complete Python projects
Understand OOP and write clean, readable code
Work with real APIs and data
Have a personalised roadmap to continue
includes: 2 weeks of doubt support via WhatsApp

variants.subject: ai
variants.title: Build with AI — Weekend Cohort
variants.tagline: Go from AI consumer to AI builder in one weekend

content.overview: A hands-on weekend that teaches you to build real products using AI APIs — not just talk about AI. By Sunday evening you'll have shipped three working projects.

sessions.title: Project 1 & 2
topics: Project 1: AI Q&A over your own documents (RAG basics)
Project 2: AI writing assistant with memory

sessions.title: Project 3 & Deployment
topics: Project 3: your own AI micro-SaaS concept
What to build next — personal roadmap

content.prerequisites: Basic Python (variables, functions, loops). No AI knowledge needed.

variants.subject: maths
variants.title: Maths for ML — Power Weekend
variants.tagline: Calculus + Linear Algebra + Probability in one focused weekend

content.overview: The complete maths toolkit for machine learning — taught in two intense, focused days. Designed for people who want to understand AI deeply, not just use it.

sessions.title: Tying it Together
topics: How all three areas combine in a neural network
Reading an ML paper's maths section

content.prerequisites: 12th grade maths (algebra, basic trigonometry). No calculus experience needed.
outcomes: Solve probability problems for ML interviews

variants.subject: science
variants.title: Classical Mechanics Weekend
variants.tagline: Newton to energy conservation — one focused weekend

content.overview: A rigorous but accessible weekend covering the core of classical mechanics. Physical intuition first — equations follow naturally. By Day 2 you'll be solving problems you never thought you could.
content.prerequisites: Basic algebra. 11th grade physics helpful but not required.
outcomes: Solve mechanics problems from first principles
Prepared for university physics or JEE/NEET mechanics

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: Build Your First Website
variants.tagline: HTML, CSS, JavaScript — from blank page to deployed site

content.overview: Four focused weeks to go from nothing to a live, deployed personal portfolio website. You build something real every session — no toy exercises.

sessions.title: JavaScript
topics: DOM manipulation — changing the page live
Fetch API — pulling data from the internet

content.prerequisites: None — absolute beginners welcome
outcomes: Build a responsive, deployed personal website
Understand HTML, CSS and JavaScript fundamentals
Use Git and deploy to Vercel
Know exactly what to learn next (React, etc.)

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: React Foundations
variants.tagline: Build modern UIs with the world's most popular frontend library

content.overview: A focused four-week sprint through React — the library that powers most modern web apps. You'll build three real projects and understand the mental model deeply.

sessions.title: React Mental Model
topics: Why React — components, JSX, the virtual DOM

content.prerequisites: HTML, CSS, basic JavaScript (variables, functions, events)
outcomes: Manage state and side effects correctly

variants.subject: python
variants.title: Core Python
variants.tagline: Core Python mastery in one focused month

content.overview: One month. One language. Complete Python competency. Covers everything from fundamentals to OOP and real projects — without the padding of a longer course.
content.prerequisites: None
outcomes: Work with APIs and data files

variants.subject: programming
variants.title: DSA Patterns
variants.tagline: Core patterns to crack coding interviews fast

content.overview: A fast, pattern-focused sprint through the DSA topics that show up most in technical interviews. One month, 15 patterns, hands-on coding every session.
content.prerequisites: Python basics (variables, loops, functions, classes)

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: SQL & Data
variants.tagline: Query, analyse and visualise data like a data engineer

content.overview: A focused sprint covering SQL from scratch to advanced queries, plus Python data analysis with pandas. Every session uses real datasets.
content.prerequisites: Basic Python helpful but not required. No SQL experience needed.

variants.subject: ai
variants.title: AI Tools
variants.tagline: Prompt engineering, APIs and agents — build real AI products

content.overview: A practical sprint on the tools and patterns driving the AI product explosion. Build five working AI projects across prompt engineering, RAG, agents, and deployment.

sessions.title: AI APIs & RAG
topics: RAG — chat over your own documents

content.prerequisites: Basic Python. No prior AI experience needed.
outcomes: Deploy AI products to the web

variants.subject: python
variants.title: Python Mastery
variants.tagline: From beginner to job-ready Python developer

content.overview: A comprehensive 90-day program built for people who want to go from zero to genuinely job-ready. Weekly live sessions, graded assignments, code reviews, and a capstone project you can show employers.
content.prerequisites: None — but commitment to 8–10 hours/week
outcomes: Build and deploy REST APIs with FastAPI
Ship a real capstone project for your portfolio
Be interview-ready for junior Python developer roles

variants.subject: programming
variants.title: DSA Deep Dive
variants.tagline: Full interview-grade DSA — every pattern, every problem type

content.overview: Eight weeks of comprehensive DSA coverage — every data structure, every algorithm pattern, and three full mock interviews. Built for people targeting product companies and top-tier tech firms.

sessions.title: Dynamic Programming
topics: Classic DP: knapsack, LCS, edit distance

sessions.title: Advanced Topics & Mock Interviews
topics: 3 full mock interviews with written feedback

content.prerequisites: Python basics. One or more of: DSA Sprint, or 50+ LeetCode problems solved.
outcomes: Perform at a high level under interview pressure
Ready for FAANG and top product company screens

variants.subject: maths
variants.title: Maths for ML — Deep Dive
variants.tagline: Calculus, Linear Algebra, Probability & Statistics — thoroughly

content.overview: Eight weeks of rigorous mathematical foundations for machine learning. Goes significantly deeper than the weekend cohort — with proofs, derivations, and applied exercises every week.

sessions.title: Probability & Statistics
topics: Probability theory — sigma algebras, random variables

sessions.title: Information Theory & Applications
topics: Capstone: derive a classic ML algorithm

content.prerequisites: 12th grade maths. Maths for ML Weekend Cohort or equivalent.
outcomes: Derive and understand core ML algorithms mathematically
Have a solid foundation for PhD-level AI work
includes: Weekly problem sets with full solutions

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: Backend Engineering
variants.tagline: APIs, databases, caching and production systems — 10 weeks

content.overview: A thorough 10-week backend engineering program. Build production-grade APIs, master database design, caching, auth, and deployment — with real projects every two weeks.

sessions.title: FastAPI & REST
topics: FastAPI in depth — routing, dependencies, middleware

sessions.title: Databases
topics: PostgreSQL in depth — schemas, indexes, transactions

content.prerequisites: Python basics. Basic understanding of APIs (HTTP, JSON).
outcomes: Build and ship production REST APIs
Deploy backend services to the cloud

variants.subject: ai
variants.title: AI & ML Engineering
variants.tagline: From fundamentals to building and deploying real models

content.overview: A rigorous 12-week program for people serious about working in AI. Code-first, maths where necessary, project-driven throughout. You will train, evaluate, and deploy real models.

sessions.title: Deep Learning
topics: CNNs for vision, RNNs/LSTMs for sequences

sessions.title: Applied AI & Capstone
topics: Capstone project: your own ML product

content.prerequisites: Python basics, algebra-level maths. No prior ML experience needed.
outcomes: Train and evaluate ML models end-to-end
Ship a real AI product for your portfolio

variants.subject: ai
variants.title: GenAI Deep Dive
variants.tagline: LLMs, RAG, agents, fine-tuning and production AI — end to end

content.overview: A comprehensive 12-week deep dive into generative AI — from how LLMs work to building and shipping production AI systems. The most complete GenAI program at this price point.
content.prerequisites: Python. Basic familiarity with AI APIs (or AI Tools Sprint).
outcomes: Build production-grade RAG and agent systems

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: Software Engineer
variants.tagline: Everything you need to land and thrive in your first software engineering role

content.overview: A comprehensive program that takes you from zero to job-ready Software Engineer. Covers programming, problem-solving, web fundamentals, Docker, cloud basics, and three real projects — everything a hiring manager expects.
content.prerequisites: None — absolute beginners welcome
outcomes: Write clean, tested Python code professionally
Use Docker and AWS for deployment
Pass technical interviews at product companies
Have 3 real projects in your portfolio

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: Senior Software Engineer
variants.tagline: Advanced engineering, system design and the skills that get you to senior

content.overview: The program that takes you to senior — covering advanced programming, system design, Kubernetes, cloud architecture, and the engineering practices that separate senior engineers from juniors.

sessions.title: System Design
topics: Case studies: URL shortener, Twitter, Uber

content.prerequisites: Software Engineer level — or equivalent 1–2 years of experience
outcomes: Pass senior-level interviews at top tech companies

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: Systems Engineer
variants.tagline: Infrastructure, cloud, automation and reliability — your foundation in systems

content.overview: A comprehensive program for people who want to work with infrastructure, cloud, and systems — rather than writing application code. Covers Linux, scripting, Docker, cloud infrastructure, databases, and monitoring.

sessions.title: Python for Automation
topics: Python scripting — files, APIs, OS interaction

sessions.title: Cloud Infrastructure
topics: AWS core services — EC2, VPC, S3, IAM

sessions.title: CI/CD & Monitoring
topics: GitHub Actions — build, test, deploy pipelines

content.prerequisites: None — curiosity about how systems work is enough
outcomes: Set up CI/CD pipelines and monitoring

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: Senior Systems Engineer
variants.tagline: Distributed systems, SRE, reliability engineering and architecture at scale

content.overview: The most advanced systems program at Qurious Academy. Covers distributed systems architecture, SRE fundamentals, reliability engineering, chaos engineering, and production operations at scale. Designed for engineers targeting Staff/Principal-level systems roles.

sessions.title: Reliability Engineering
topics: Resilience patterns — circuit breakers, retries, bulkheads

sessions.title: Architecture & Leadership
topics: Capstone: design a production system from scratch

content.prerequisites: Systems Engineer or equivalent 3+ years in infrastructure/platform engineering
outcomes: Design and operate high-availability distributed systems
Lead SRE practices across engineering teams
Run chaos experiments and improve system resilience

variants.subject: ai
variants.title: AI Engineer
variants.tagline: Train, deploy and maintain AI systems — from fundamentals to production

content.overview: A complete program for engineers who want to build real AI systems — not just use AI APIs. Covers machine learning, deep learning, cloud AI services, and MLOps, with three real projects you can deploy.

sessions.title: Deep Learning
topics: CNNs for vision, transformers for NLP

content.prerequisites: Python basics. Some maths background (12th grade level).
outcomes: Train and evaluate ML models end-to-end
Build deep learning systems with PyTorch

content.overview: The fastest path to becoming a GenAI specialist. Four months covering everything from LLM fundamentals to building and deploying production-grade RAG systems, agents, and fine-tuned models.

sessions.title: LLMs & Prompt Engineering
topics: How LLMs work — transformers, attention, tokenization

sessions.title: Agents & Fine-Tuning
topics: Agent patterns — tool use, planning, memory

Deploy GenAI products to the web
Evaluate and monitor AI output quality

variants.subject: ai
variants.title: Senior AI Engineer
variants.tagline: Advanced ML, MLOps, multi-modal AI and AI at production scale

content.overview: A rigorous 9-month program for engineers who want to move from working with AI to leading AI initiatives. Covers advanced architectures, MLOps, multi-modal systems, and research-to-production workflows.

sessions.title: Advanced Deep Learning
topics: Attention mechanisms — MHA, cross-attention, flash attention

sessions.title: MLOps & Model Production
topics: Model serving at scale — Ray Serve, Triton

sessions.title: Leadership & Capstone
topics: Capstone: production AI system at scale

content.prerequisites: AI Engineer or equivalent — 2+ years building ML systems
outcomes: Design and operate ML systems at production scale

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: MTS I
variants.tagline: Target entry-level roles at product companies and big tech

content.overview: Purpose-built for getting an MTS I (equivalent to L3/L4 at big tech) offer. Combines advanced DSA, system design basics, behavioural interview prep, and multiple mock interviews — everything calibrated to the bar that top tech companies set.

sessions.title: Advanced DSA
topics: All major patterns — sliding window, two pointers, graphs, DP

sessions.title: System Design Fundamentals
topics: Design: URL shortener, social feed, chat app

sessions.title: Mock Interviews & Refinement
topics: 5 full mock coding interviews with feedback

content.prerequisites: Software Engineer level knowledge or 1+ year of coding experience
outcomes: Solve hard DSA problems under time pressure
Land MTS I / L3 / L4 offers at top tech companies

variants.title: MTS II
variants.tagline: Target mid-level roles at big tech — L5 and above

content.overview: A rigorous 9-month program for engineers targeting L5 / MTS II at major tech companies. Goes deep on distributed systems, advanced system design, performance engineering, and the leadership signals senior interviewers look for.
content.prerequisites: MTS I level or 3+ years at a product company
outcomes: Pass L5/MTS II bars at FAANG & top tech

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: MTS III
variants.tagline: Senior / L6 interviews — technical leadership and architecture mastery

content.overview: The most intensive interview prep program at Qurious Academy — designed for engineers targeting L6 / Senior Staff and above. Covers architectural decision-making, cross-team technical strategy, executive communication, and L6-calibrated mock interviews.

sessions.title: Architecture at Scale
topics: Technical debt management at org scale

sessions.title: Technical Strategy
topics: Build vs buy vs open-source decisions

sessions.title: L6 System Design & Coding
topics: Advanced coding — hard problems under pressure

outcomes: Make and defend architectural decisions at scale

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: Staff Software Engineer
variants.tagline: Org-level technical leadership, architecture and staff-level interview mastery

content.overview: A 12-month program for senior engineers aiming at Staff / L7 roles — where the job is no longer just about writing code, but about shaping engineering direction across teams. Covers technical roadmapping, architectural deep-dives, Staff-level interview prep, and real leadership case studies.

sessions.title: Org-Level System Design
topics: Designing for org boundaries and team topology
Migration strategy for large legacy systems
Staff-level system design case studies (Stripe, Airbnb)

sessions.title: Technical Roadmapping
topics: Prioritisation frameworks for tech debt vs features

sessions.title: Staff Interview Prep & Capstone
topics: Staff-level coding & system design (Google L7, Stripe, Cloudflare style)
Capstone: produce a real engineering strategy doc

content.prerequisites: MTS III level or 7+ years with demonstrated technical leadership
outcomes: Shape engineering direction across multiple teams
Write and drive org-level technical strategy
Pass Staff / L7 interviews at top tech companies
Mentor and grow senior engineering talent

variants.subject: python
variants.title: Python for Data Science and Machine Learning
variants.tagline: NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib and the full data workflow

content.overview: A structured 8-week program covering the complete Python data science stack — from raw data to actionable insights. Project-driven: every week ends with a real dataset.

sessions.title: Pandas — Data Wrangling
topics: Project: clean a messy real-world dataset

sessions.title: Visualisation & EDA
topics: Project: full EDA on Indian census data

content.prerequisites: Basic Python (variables, loops, functions). No statistics background needed.
outcomes: Clean, transform and analyse real datasets with Pandas
Present findings clearly in Jupyter notebooks

variants.subject: programming
variants.title: Go
variants.tagline: Build fast, concurrent systems with Go — one focused month

content.overview: A focused month on Go — the language built for simplicity and concurrency. Covers the full language, concurrency patterns, and building real networked services.
content.prerequisites: One programming language at a basic level (Python, Java, or C)
outcomes: Build concurrent programs with goroutines & channels

variants.subject: programming
variants.title: Java
variants.tagline: Core Java, OOP and the JVM — one focused month

content.overview: A complete Java foundation in one month. Covers the language, object-oriented design, the JVM, and building a real Spring Boot API project.

sessions.title: Java Foundations
topics: Control flow — if, for, while, switch

content.prerequisites: None — basic programming logic helpful
outcomes: Build REST APIs with Spring Boot
Understand the JVM and Java ecosystem

variants.subject: programming
variants.title: C
variants.tagline: The language that runs the world — memory, pointers, and systems

content.overview: A focused month on C — the foundation of systems programming. Learn memory management, pointers, and how computers actually work under the hood.

sessions.title: Systems C
topics: Capstone: build a mini memory allocator

content.prerequisites: Basic programming logic (any language). Curiosity about how computers work.
outcomes: Understand pointers and manual memory management
Build a foundation for OS and embedded programming

variants.subject: programming
variants.title: C++
variants.tagline: Modern C++ for high-performance programming

content.overview: Modern C++ (C++17/20) in one month. Covers OOP, the STL, move semantics, and smart pointers — the features that make C++ both powerful and manageable.

sessions.title: OOP & Templates
topics: STL containers — vector, map, set, deque

content.prerequisites: C basics (or C Sprint). Basic understanding of pointers and memory.
outcomes: Use STL containers and algorithms effectively
Manage memory safely with smart pointers

variants.subject: science
variants.title: Chemistry
variants.tagline: Core chemistry concepts — actually understood

content.overview: A focused 3-hour session covering the core ideas in chemistry — atomic structure, chemical bonding, and reaction types — built around intuition and real-world examples.

sessions.title: Atomic Structure & Periodicity
topics: Periodic table trends — ionisation energy, electronegativity
Why elements behave the way they do

sessions.title: Reactions & Applications
topics: Types of reactions — synthesis, decomposition, redox

content.prerequisites: Basic school-level science. No prior chemistry knowledge needed.

variants.subject: science
variants.title: Organic Chemistry Weekend
variants.tagline: Mechanisms, reactions and molecular thinking — one focused weekend

content.overview: Two intensive days on organic chemistry — the chemistry of carbon. Covers naming, functional groups, reaction mechanisms, and stereochemistry with a focus on pattern recognition over memorisation.

sessions.title: Structure & Functional Groups
topics: Functional groups — alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, alcohols, carbonyls

sessions.title: Reaction Mechanisms
topics: SN1 vs SN2 — when and why

content.prerequisites: General chemistry basics (equivalent to 11th grade). Atomic structure & bonding.

variants.subject: technology
variants.title: Interview Preparation
variants.tagline: Targeted prep for your specific role, stack and timeline

content.overview: Personalised interview preparation built around your target role, tech stack and timeline. Covers DSA, system design, and behavioural rounds — calibrated to the specific company and level you're targeting.

sessions.title: Role Assessment & Gap Analysis
topics: Your current level vs target role bar
Identifying weak areas in DSA, design, or behavioural
Custom study plan based on your timeline

sessions.title: DSA Targeted Practice
topics: Company-specific DSA patterns (based on Glassdoor, LeetCode discuss)
Weekly problem sets from your target company's question bank

sessions.title: System Design
topics: HLD calibrated to your target level
Design problems commonly asked at your target company

content.prerequisites: Basic coding skills. Works best if you have a specific company or role in mind.
outcomes: Perform at your target company's interview bar
Clear coding, design and behavioural rounds confidently
Get a structured feedback loop to improve quickly
